title: Milk Money

jokes.text: They call this a candidates' breakfast?!
jokes.setup: Moira arrives at what should be a standard political event
jokes.punchline: Her immediate disdain and shock at the normalcy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moira's entitled shock at ordinary events

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What is this? A... a municipal gathering? In a church basement? With folding chairs and store-bought refreshments?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've attended galas on yachts with less tragic energy than this.

jokes.text: Well, it can't be, Moira, there's a woman in cargo shorts.
jokes.setup: Johnny responding to Moira's complaint about the event
jokes.punchline: Using cargo shorts as evidence of low-class status
jokes.explanation: Sharp class-conscious humor that's both character-specific and economical

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want me to do about it? I'm not going to wear cargo shorts.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's exactly the problem.

jokes.text: Oh, bless you, dear, but a name tag does not go with this ensemble.
jokes.setup: Twyla offers Moira a standard name tag
jokes.punchline: Moira treats it as a fashion emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - treating mundane things as incompatible with her grandeur

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Here's your name tag.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A name tag? Twyla, this is a fashion emergency. Do you have any idea what this will do to my silhouette? It's going to create an unfortunate visual interruption right at the bust line.

jokes.text: Not that people won't know, when they see you in that outfit. It's very shiny.
jokes.setup: Twyla explaining why people would recognize Moira
jokes.punchline: Her innocent, literal description of Moira's outrageous outfit
jokes.explanation: Twyla's sweet obliviousness to how ridiculous Moira looks

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, she was wearing a large feathered headdress, and a sequined bodysuit that was mostly sheer, with like, seven-inch platform heels, and she had glitter all over her face.

jokes.text: Well, I'm glad you talked me out of the Mohawk.
jokes.setup: Moira reflecting on her outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she considered a Mohawk for a political event
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd - the idea that a Mohawk was even considered shows how disconnected Moira is

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I considered a Mohawk for the political event, but I thought it might be too subtle.

jokes.text: I was always impressed by those masquerade key parties at the Sarkosy's.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her inspiration for bringing glamour to politics
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing swinger parties as her template
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira's oblivious reference to key parties as inspiration

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I thought, 'What if I brought a little glamour to politics?' And then I remembered those parties in the Hamptons, you know, the... social gatherings. Very inclusive. Everyone was welcome. Very open-minded. And I thought, 'That's the template.'

jokes.text: And Elvis! I'm so glad you could make it.
jokes.setup: Roland greeting someone at the political breakfast
jokes.punchline: Calling Moira 'Elvis' due to her outfit choice
jokes.explanation: Roland's tactless comparison creates cringe humor, though it's a fairly obvious joke

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, well, well, look who's here.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Moira, you're looking very... Elvis today.

jokes.text: Well, because you look like Elvis in that... outfit there, you know, and... but the early Elvis, not the...
jokes.setup: Roland comparing Moira to Elvis
jokes.punchline: His awkward backpedaling when he realizes he's insulting her
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Roland digs himself deeper

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, Moira, you're a lot like Elvis.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know, the whole... the whole Vegas thing, the jumpsuits, the, uh... the way people just kind of stare at you.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Not in a bad way! I mean, Elvis was... he was very loved. Very respected. Although, you know, towards the end, he did let himself go a little bit, and—no, no, I'm not saying you're letting yourself go. You look great. You always look great. I just meant the legacy thing. You know, how some people remember you one way and then...

jokes.text: Hah! You are a hit!
jokes.setup: Johnny responding to people talking about Moira's outfit
jokes.punchline: Calling negative attention a 'hit'
jokes.explanation: Basic ironic humor - Johnny trying to spin negative attention positively

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, she's getting attention, isn't she? I call that a hit.

jokes.text: straight from the cow's breast, to your mouth
jokes.setup: Bob explaining the quality of his raw milk
jokes.punchline: Calling udders 'breasts' in an oddly sensual way
jokes.explanation: Bob's awkward way of describing milk creates unintended innuendo

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: This raw milk comes from cows with the most beautiful breasts.

jokes.text: maybe you're used to being on the wrong side of the law, but I for one am not going to prison.
jokes.setup: Johnny treating raw milk like a serious crime
jokes.punchline: Acting like Bob is a hardened criminal over milk
jokes.explanation: Johnny's dramatic escalation of the milk situation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Bob, do you understand the gravity of what you've done? Raw milk is incredibly dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: It's just milk from a local farm, Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: JUST milk? Bob, you're a criminal. A hardened criminal. I'm going to have to distance myself from you.

jokes.text: Gwen has already told me she won't wait for me
jokes.setup: Bob treating milk selling like serious crime
jokes.punchline: His girlfriend won't wait if he goes to prison for milk
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - treating milk like a serious crime with real consequences

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: If I go to prison for milk, my girlfriend won't wait for me.

jokes.text: Right, okay Bob, I'll keep it on the down low.
jokes.setup: Johnny agreeing to be discrete about milk
jokes.punchline: Using drug dealer language for dairy products
jokes.explanation: Johnny adopting criminal terminology for innocent milk transaction

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm very discrete. I can keep a secret about milk.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You need some whole milk? I know a guy. He's got the good stuff, keeps it in the back. Very fresh. Moves it fast.

jokes.text: I am approachable...
jokes.setup: Moira seeking validation about her personality
jokes.punchline: Stating it as fact rather than asking a question
jokes.explanation: Moira's inability to actually ask for feedback shows her lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not difficult, I'm just particular. There's a difference.

jokes.text: That sounds more like a fact that you're sharing with me.
jokes.setup: David responding to Moira's non-question
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the logical flaw in her approach
jokes.explanation: David's dry observation about his mother's communication style

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's not really a question, so I'm not sure how you expect me to answer it.

jokes.text: Stevie! Stevie! You're an average Joe... Anne.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to relate to Stevie as a common person
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly gender-correcting the phrase 'average Joe'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - overthinking simple expressions and making them awkward

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, Stevie, I'm just like you. I'm an average... Jane.

jokes.text: You sound a bit like a dictator.
jokes.setup: Moira talking about leading and masses following
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - cutting through Moira's grandiosity with brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, the masses are also following a guy on TikTok who eats cereal with orange juice, so.

jokes.text: As a strong willed modern woman, with a high school degree, you know how hard we have to work to be taken seriously!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to relate to Stevie
jokes.punchline: Moira's condescending assumption about Stevie's education level
jokes.explanation: Moira's attempt at solidarity while being accidentally insulting

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, Stevie, I'm going to simplify this for you. When I was your age, I was already fluent in three languages. Well, two and a half if you count my brief Esperanto phase. But the point is, education is really about expanding one's vocabulary and cultural references.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Moira, I went to college.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, a community college, dear. That's very... resourceful of you.

jokes.text: As a strong willed modern woman, with a high school degree, you know how hard we have to work to be taken seriously!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to relate to Stevie's struggles
jokes.punchline: Condescendingly assuming Stevie only has a high school education
jokes.explanation: Moira's unconscious classism while trying to be relatable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you know, Stevie, I went to a very prestigious boarding school in Switzerland. We had a curriculum that was quite rigorous. But I'm sure your high school experience was... quaint in its own way.

jokes.text: I actually went to college, but sure.
jokes.setup: Stevie correcting Moira's assumption
jokes.punchline: Her dry acceptance of the insult
jokes.explanation: Stevie's perfect deflation of Moira's condescension

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Is that your girlfriend?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, that's my business partner.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, so she's a lesbian too?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, she's just my business partner.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's still very modern.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, thanks for the compliment... I think.

jokes.text: The queen hasn't smiled since the 70's, and her birthdays are still very well attended.
jokes.setup: David defending lack of approachability
jokes.punchline: Using the Queen's stern demeanor as a positive example
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy that's both specific and character-appropriate for David

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not unapproachable. I'm just... discerning about who I interact with. The Queen of England has the same demeanor, and people seem to respect her just fine.

jokes.text: Like, really good, like, super... sun kissed, and muscle-y.
jokes.setup: Alexis seeing Ted after their breakup
jokes.punchline: Her inability to hide her attraction despite the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Alexis's transparent attempt to be casual while clearly attracted

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, hi! I didn't know you'd be here.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was just... I had to pick up some files.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right. Of course. That makes total sense. You look... I mean, your hair looks... Did you do something different?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, it's the same.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, no, I knew that. I just meant... anyway. So how have you been? Because I've been great. Really great. Not thinking about you at all.

jokes.text: 'Cause they actually gave me the 'Broken Hearts Special.' Three extra nights at no extra charge, as long as I could prove that I didn't actually make it down the aisle.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining what happened to their vacation
jokes.punchline: A resort package specifically for jilted grooms
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny concept - resorts capitalizing on heartbreak

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So I booked us this amazing resort package. Turns out it was specifically for jilted grooms.

jokes.text: Yeah! Yeah, no, I got almost full price back for the engagement ring, and I've always wanted one of these, and it cost less than the ring, so...
jokes.setup: Ted explaining his new motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Turning his broken engagement into a financial win
jokes.explanation: Ted finding silver lining in heartbreak through practical economics

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I sold my engagement ring to buy this motorcycle. So really, Sheila breaking up with me was the best investment I ever made.

jokes.text: It's like if the 'Sons of Anarchy' had a vet.
jokes.setup: Alexis seeing Ted in motorcycle gear
jokes.punchline: Combining tough biker image with gentle veterinarian
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of contrasting images

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ted, you look so... cool.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah? I picked up a motorcycle.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's so sexy. I didn't know you had a dangerous side.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I'm taking it slow. I mostly just ride to the animal clinic.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Except they don't wear helmets on that show, Which I get, but, it's a little dangerous.
jokes.setup: Ted responding to the Sons of Anarchy comparison
jokes.punchline: His safety-conscious veterinarian perspective on biker culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ted character moment - practical safety concerns about TV drama

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, bikers have a very high rate of head injuries, and most of them don't wear helmets, which is just... it's irresponsible. And the leather — sure, it looks cool, but it's not going to protect you from much. And don't even get me started on the motorcycles themselves. Do you know how many accidents I see? It's a lot.

jokes.text: Granted, I bought it from a man who I later found out was a drifter.
jokes.setup: Twyla explaining her bad milk experience
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing she bought milk from a suspicious source
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - innocent Twyla having sketchy milk dealer

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, I bought it from a guy in a van in the Walmart parking lot.

jokes.text: It might've been elk's milk. Is elk's milk a thing? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Twyla continuing her milk story
jokes.punchline: Innocent uncertainty about whether elk can be milked
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of the milk story with Twyla's naive delivery

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I was just thinking about the time my dad tried to milk an elk. I'm not actually sure if that's something you can do with an elk. Can you milk an elk?

jokes.text: You would make a terrible drug dealer, Mr. Rose.
jokes.setup: Johnny being obvious about seeking illegal milk
jokes.punchline: Sweet Twyla matter-of-factly assessing his criminal potential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla moment - innocent observation delivered with deadpan honesty

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm looking for milk. Fresh milk. The good stuff. You know what I'm talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: You'd be a terrible drug dealer, Mr. Rose.

jokes.text: I mean, I get it, it's fast money, and no one would ever suspect you.
jokes.setup: Alexis thinking Johnny wants to be a drug dealer
jokes.punchline: Her casual expertise about drug dealing logistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis - casual familiarity with illegal activities

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying you want to start small, maybe work your way up to a larger operation? Because honestly, Dad, the key is consistency. You need reliable suppliers, a solid customer base, and you definitely can't be doing business out of the same location every time. That's like, Drug Dealing 101.

jokes.text: I had a friend in Venice Beach who sold raw milk, and his entire compound was raided.
jokes.setup: Alexis giving advice about raw milk business
jokes.punchline: Treating milk selling like a major criminal enterprise
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - milk selling as serious crime with 'compounds'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know, if you're going to sell raw milk, you have to treat it like a major criminal enterprise. You need lookouts, code words, a whole distribution network.

jokes.text: your trail of discarded boyfriends, Alexis, is making it very difficult to get anything done in this world.
jokes.setup: Johnny frustrated about needing to contact Ted
jokes.punchline: Treating Alexis's dating history as a practical business problem
jokes.explanation: Johnny's practical frustration with his daughter's romantic chaos

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I need to get in touch with Ted. Do you have his number?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Um, yeah, I think so. I dated him for like three weeks in 2015.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, can you just call him?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, I've dated a lot of people. I can't just call every guy I've ever been with.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Why not? You seem to have kept in touch with most of them.

jokes.text: For what? I didn't do anything.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn thanking David for helping with outfit selection
jokes.punchline: David's plausible deniability about helping the opposition
jokes.explanation: David's careful deflection to maintain deniability

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for helping me pick out this outfit, David. You have such great taste.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate that, but I should mention that I have no recollection of this interaction, and if anyone asks, I was not here.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: What? Why would you say that?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because you're wearing it to support your husband's business, and I have a reputation to protect.

jokes.text: The twelve 'things' of milk?
jokes.setup: Alexis describing what she brought as 'things'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's horrified realization of the miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the milk quantity disaster

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You brought things? Alexis, we discussed this. I asked you to bring necessities. Basic necessities.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I did bring necessities, Dad. These are things that I need.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You brought your winter shoe collection to a motel room in a town we may only be in for a few weeks.

jokes.text: 'Cause I was wondering why would Dad order $300.00 worth of milk?
jokes.setup: Alexis revealing the cost
jokes.punchline: Her innocent confusion about the excessive quantity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the financial disaster

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, how much did you spend?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: On what?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Candles.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: For the store?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, for the store.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: How many candles are we talking?

jokes.text: Um... okay well, $394.40, so I guess kind of closer to $400.
jokes.setup: Johnny shocked at $300
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually correcting to an even worse number
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation - making bad news worse with casual precision

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Three hundred dollars?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, three thousand.

jokes.text: I try and do something nice for somebody, You think I would at least deserve a 'thank you!'
jokes.setup: Johnny angry about the milk disaster
jokes.punchline: Alexis genuinely believing she deserves gratitude for the catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - expecting praise for creating a disaster

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Alexis, you destroyed the entire milk supply! Do you understand what you've done?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Daddy, I know, but honestly, I think you should be thanking me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Thanking you? For what?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I could have just left it there, but instead I cleaned it up. You're welcome.

jokes.text: Just remember, if things go south, there's a loaded BB gun in the back.
jokes.setup: Roland preparing for dangerous milk transaction
jokes.punchline: His weapon of choice is a BB gun
jokes.explanation: Roland's pathetic attempt at being intimidating

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm going in armed. Got my BB gun right here.

jokes.text: You're acting like I've never done this before!
jokes.setup: Roland panicking during the checkpoint
jokes.punchline: Claiming experience while clearly having no idea what he's doing
jokes.explanation: Roland's delusional confidence in his criminal abilities

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I've run dozens of checkpoints. Hundreds maybe. I know exactly what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Where's the... the checkpoint thing? And what am I looking for again?

jokes.text: I remember I just got my braces off, so...
jokes.setup: Johnny shocked Alexis was in Johannesburg at 14
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually dating her dangerous travels by orthodontic milestones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - casual reference to dangerous teen adventures

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I was in Johannesburg when I was 14, but that was before I got my braces off, so it was really more of a phase.

jokes.text: What do you think her name was? - I don't know. - Gayle! The man marries three Gayles!
jokes.setup: Ronnie setting up story about Uncle Ralph's marriages
jokes.punchline: All three wives had the same name
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with absurd coincidence

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: All three wives had the same name

jokes.text: Like didn't learn anything from the first two! I mean, what is that?!
jokes.setup: The revelation about three wives named Gayle
jokes.punchline: Twyla's exasperated commentary on the pattern
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the setup, Twyla's genuine exasperation sells the absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. You had three wives, and they were all named Gayle?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: That's not a pattern, that's a fetish.

jokes.text: It takes a real eye to find something of quality at a clothier in Elmdale.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn looking good in new outfit
jokes.punchline: Moira's backhanded compliment about the store's quality
jokes.explanation: Moira's attempt to compliment while still being condescending about local shopping

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, you look absolutely radiant. Is that from the store? Well, I mean, it certainly looks expensive.

jokes.text: that's uh, Jennifer. And then he's um... Jacob. And then I'm also Jacob.
jokes.setup: Roland giving fake names to police
jokes.punchline: Using the same fake name for himself and Johnny
jokes.explanation: Roland's panicked incompetence at lying

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Officer, the name's Buck Henry.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Wait, that's my name too?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I wasn't going to give you a good one.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm just joking. We don't have drivers' licences. Um... No, yeah, we do have driver's licenses, we don't have those names on our driver's license.
jokes.setup: Roland's fake name situation getting worse
jokes.punchline: His attempt to clarify making everything more confusing and suspicious
jokes.explanation: Roland's continued panic-digging creates escalating cringe as he makes things worse

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no, no, you don't understand. My name isn't actually Roland Schitt.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's... it's Roland Schitt. That's the real one. The other one was fake.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What? Why are you looking at me like that? I'm explaining myself!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The fake name was also Roland Schitt, but spelled different. With a 'y.' Rolynd.

jokes.text: Actually, my birth name is Angelica.
jokes.setup: Roland calling her Jennifer as fake name
jokes.punchline: Alexis making the lie more complicated
jokes.explanation: Alexis adding unnecessary complexity to the deception

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Actually, it's Jennifer Rose. Jennifer Rose Goldstein. My parents are both doctors, and I grew up in Connecticut, and I went to Brown.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm actually a competitive figure skater, and I'm just taking a year off to find myself.

jokes.text: You farm in that suit?
jokes.setup: Johnny claiming to be a dairy farmer
jokes.punchline: Officer noting his inappropriate attire
jokes.explanation: Officer's logical observation exposing their deception

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: You're a dairy farmer in a Valentino suit?

jokes.text: When the weather's good.
jokes.setup: Officer questioning Johnny's farming attire
jokes.punchline: Johnny's ridiculous excuse for wearing a suit while farming
jokes.explanation: Johnny's desperate attempt to maintain the farmer lie

jokes.text: You farm in that suit?
jokes.setup: Johnny claiming to be a dairy farmer
jokes.punchline: Officer noticing his inappropriate attire for farming
jokes.explanation: Officer's logical question exposing the obvious flaw in their story

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: I'm a dairy farmer.

dialogue.speaker: Woman Officer
dialogue.line: You're wearing a cashmere sweater.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: Yes, well, it's a very nice dairy farm.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm-I'm sorry, I thought you were talking to the other Jacob.
jokes.setup: Officer calling for Jacob to pull over
jokes.punchline: Roland confused by his own fake name system
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier fake name confusion

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Pull over, Jacob Jingleheimer Schmidt!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Jacob Jingleheimer Schmidt? That's not— wait, that's my fake name system. How do you know my fake name system?

jokes.text: No, she looked like an ageing stewardess from a Latvian airline!
jokes.setup: Moira criticizing Jocelyn's outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific and dramatic insult
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - incredibly specific, dramatic, and oddly sophisticated insult

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, that ensemble looks like it was curated by a woman who shops exclusively at gas stations and believes that fabric softener is a personality trait.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I think you'll find one lodged in the middle of my back.
jokes.setup: David asking about getting a knife
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic metaphor about being betrayed
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira melodrama - turning a simple request into betrayal metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A knife? David, a knife is what you use when someone has already stabbed you in the back. It's the tool of the betrayed, the weapon of the wounded. If you're asking me for a knife, then you've already lost the war.

jokes.text: Oh my God, she didn't know which way was up when I was done with her!
jokes.setup: Roland defending his performance with the police
jokes.punchline: His delusional assessment of his terrible lying
jokes.explanation: Roland's complete misreading of how badly he handled the situation

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I was very convincing. I looked them right in the eye, didn't break character for a second.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I think I really threw them off with that bit where I said 'I definitely wasn't there' three times in a row.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And when I started sweating profusely and my eye twitched, I'm pretty sure that sealed it. Very authentic stuff.

jokes.text: Someone has to find naked pictures of me. Better you than a stranger in a storm cellar.
jokes.setup: Someone needs to search for compromising photos
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and disturbing alternative scenario
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with disturbing but creative imagery

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We need to search for those compromising photos before they surface.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I suppose we could hire someone discrete. Though the last time we hired help, they turned out to be running a side business selling our vintage clothing to consignment shops.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not helpful, Mother.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm simply saying, David, that in this family we've learned that when you try to contain a problem, it usually results in someone's intimate apparel ending up on eBay with a detailed provenance.

jokes.text: Someone has to find naked pictures of me. Better you than a stranger in a storm cellar.
jokes.setup: Someone needs to search for nude photos
jokes.punchline: The darkly specific alternative scenario of a stranger in a storm cellar
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with very specific imagery - 'stranger in a storm cellar' is memorably ominous

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: We need to search for nude photos of myself on the internet.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick Brewer
dialogue.line: Why would there be nude photos of you on the internet?

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: I don't know, Patrick. Why would there be a stranger in a storm cellar?

tmdb_overview: Johnny gets thrown into the raw milk business. Moira tries to connect with the townspeople. David gives style advice.
insight: Steady character comedy carries 55 jokes at 2.35 per minute, hitting franchise baseline.
